meaning: n.[亦作splendor] 光彩壮丽,显赫,杰出
examples: The palace has been restored to its former splendour.
She gazed down upon the nighttime splendour of the city.
The film is directed with a technical bravura and visual splendour.
They are fine places to contemplate the splendour of the palace and the lives of its inhabitants.
Filing into the turquoise splendour of the Mariinsky Theatre they were braced for the worst.
